cases.input: Our contract-review agent emits findings with a raw confidence score. We cut at confidence >= 0.7 and report 88% aggregate accuracy. Design the evaluation: calibrate confidence against our 600-item labeled set, stratify by document_type, and report false-positive rate per category.
/evaluation-confidence-design calibrate the classifier threshold and add stratified sampling by document_type.
We need confidence calibration and false positive criteria for our evaluator, but no one has fit a calibration map yet.

We have a labeled set of 600 findings tagged by document_type (contract, NDA, DPA) and category (indemnity, termination, data_privacy, payment_terms). data_privacy on NDA shows many false positives but aggregate accuracy looks fine. Build calibrated cutoff, stratified sampling, categorical criteria with +/- examples per severity, FP rate per category, and a temporal disable for noisy categories.
Our evaluation uses a single global random sample, so rare document_types disappear from the metric. Make the sampling stratified with a minimum per stratum.
Someone fit an isotonic calibration map on only 40 labels and the curve is non-monotonic. Should we apply this map and cut on it?
Help me write release notes for this sprint.
Just cut on the raw confidence score and report one aggregate accuracy number; skip calibration and per-category FP.
Train a new classifier model and label raw documents from scratch.
Complete missing files for evaluation-confidence-design without overwriting local edits.
Create a local experimental evaluation-confidence-design variant under .local.
